old_claim.note: Sailing provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct trade_routes to sailing dependency remains because the trade route node is anchored to broad early exchange networks around -8000, while corrected source-backed sailing is later Naqada II evidence around -3300.

source_shape.source_locator: The History of Money is used on trade_routes for barter and early exchange context; it does not make sail propulsion a prerequisite for early trade routes.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports early exchange and barter context without requiring sail-powered watercraft.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Since source-backed sailing is now much later than the -8000 trade_routes node, retaining sailing as a prerequisite would create false chronology and overstate maritime technology.

ontology_before: Trade Routes depended directly on sailing, implying broad early exchange networks required sail propulsion despite a much earlier trade-route date.
ontology_after: Trade Routes remains a broad land-and-water exchange node without a direct sailing prerequisite; later maritime commerce can depend on sailing separately.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: trade_routes no longer depends directly on sailing.
would_reject_if: The trade_routes node is rescoped to late Predynastic or later maritime trade routes only.
A source shows the -8000 trade-route evidence specifically required sail-powered boats.
The sailing node is moved back to a source-backed date earlier than trade_routes.
short_reason: Early trade routes predate the corrected source-backed sailing node and should not depend on it.
